cq_social_dynamic_packstar.comment: The * suffix means "high probability but not certain". Applies generally to all 4 categories (pack*, group*, lone*, unknown*), not only pack*. Saved as project-memory grammar rule. step1d_dataqc.py updated to accept all 8 valid values.

cq_standalone_question_mark.comment: Decision: standalone ? (not as suffix) becomes "unknown". Applied to pack_name (5 wolves: F22, Sh51, Sh54, O73, O77) and seen with (3 wolves: Y32, Y102, Sl60). Total 8 cells changed.

cq_lone_wolves_within_threshold.comment: Re-ran with stricter criterion (both endpoints + SAME camera per 2026-05-13 ruling): 6 of 8 wolves now CONFIRMED lone — algorithm agrees with your classification. (H15m: cams 22+28 in makhfi, makhfi new is at cam 29 — different camera. Y101: cam 6, yehodiya trio at cams 7+10 — different cameras. Y28: cam 7, golden pack at cams 8+9+10 — different. Sh59: cam 19, Shaal west* at cam 20 — different. Sl65: cam 48, slopes* at cams 46+47 — different. O66: cam 31, Odem East at cams 32+34+35 — different.) ONLY 2 wolves still ambiguous (see new dedicated questions): Y46 (cam 10, matches both golden pack via Y39 AND yehodiya trio via Y47), Y37 (cam 7, matches both dark pack via Y29 AND yehodiya trio via Y48). The polygon-overlap matches I originally suggested were coincidental — different cameras = different physical locations.

cq_sh57_sole_shaal_west_star.comment: RESOLVED by 2026-05-13 rule: Shaal west and Shaal west* are the SAME pack. Sh57 with pack name=Shaal west* is correctly a probable member of Shaal west (2-wolf pack: Sh51 confirmed + Sh57 probable). No data change needed.
